post.description: Use this endpoint to obtain a connect token, which can be used to initialize the Meld widget and start a bank linking connection.

400.description: Bad Request : check request values and format

403.description: Unauthenticated or authenticated with insufficient access

500.description: Server Error. This applies to all 5xx errors, including but not limited to 500, 501, 502, 503, and 504 errors. You should treat all of these errors the same.

post.description: A Service Provider's connection to the institution may sometimes degrade or new financial account(s) may be discovered for the connection. In order to fix the connection or add these newly discovered account(s), the customer must reconnect. This endpoint generates a new connect token for the connection to enable this reconnection process. This connect token can be used to invoke a new bank linking flow where the user can enter the service provider's UI to fix the connection

201.description: Generated new connect token for repairing this connection

400.description: Bad Request : check request values and format

403.description: Unauthenticated or authenticated with insufficient access

500.description: Server Error. This applies to all 5xx errors, including but not limited to 500, 501, 502, 503, and 504 errors. You should treat all of these errors the same.

get.description: Retrieve details of a specific institution connection.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: Connection id

400.description: Bad Request : check request values and format

403.description: Unauthenticated or authenticated with insufficient access

500.description: Server Error. This applies to all 5xx errors, including but not limited to 500, 501, 502, 503, and 504 errors. You should treat all of these errors the same.

delete.description:
Use this endpoint to delete a connection and all of its financial accounts. This will also delete the connection with the service provider used to make the connection, thus stopping any updates for the connection and its financial accounts. Listen for the associated BANK_LINKING_CONNECTION_DELETED

webhook for acknowledgement that the connection was deleted.

Note: Financial accounts may belong to multiple connections. This can occur if a customer connects to their institution multiple times in separate sessions, either through the same or a different service provider. If a connection is deleted but its financial accounts still belong to another active connection, then they won't be deleted yet. Not until all the connections that a financial account is associated with are deleted, will the financial account then be deleted.

Note: In some cases, connections will be deleted before ever calling this endpoint. The aforementioned webhook will notify when this occurs, and calling this endpoint for the connection will no longer be necessary. This can occur if a customer stops granting access to the service provider directly through their institution.

Note: Duplicate provider connections occur when the same customer connects to the same institution using the same login credentials through the same service provider. In such cases, only the most recently refreshed of these duplicates is actively maintained and the rest are considered inactive. When deleting duplicate connections, all of the inactive duplicates must be deleted prior to deleting the main duplicate. If an attempt is made to delete the active duplicate prior to the inactive duplicates being deleted, then the next most recently aggregated duplicate will become the active one. Only once all duplicates are deleted will this sever the connection with the service provider and cease billing. Duplicate connections made through different service providers do not have this restriction, however.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: Connection id

400.description: Bad Request : check request values and format

403.description: Unauthenticated or authenticated with insufficient access

500.description: Server Error. This applies to all 5xx errors, including but not limited to 500, 501, 502, 503, and 504 errors. You should treat all of these errors the same.

get.description: Retrieve institution connections filtered by various parameters.

parameters.name: customerId
parameters.description: Meld generated unique identifier for your customer. This should be used to track customer activity.

parameters.name: externalCustomerId
parameters.description: Your unique identifier for your customer. If maintaining your own customer management system this can also be used for tracking customer activity.

parameters.name: institutionId
parameters.description: Institution id. If present, only institution connections with this institution will be included.

parameters.name: statuses
parameters.description:
Comma separated list of institution connection statuses. If present, only institution connections with these statuses will be included.

Find out more about connection statuses here.

parameters.name: serviceProviders
parameters.description:
Comma separated list of service providers. If present, only institution connections with these service providers will be included.

Note: Reference Supported Service Providers for the list of available bank linking providers.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start date to begin search from of format yyyy-mm-dd

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End date stop search on of format yyyy-mm-dd

parameters.name: before
parameters.description:
Unique key corresponding to an institution connection's position in the paginated list of results -- used to indicate that only institution connections residing before this key will be retrieved.

Each institution connection carries a unique key. Reference Pagination

The before and after fields cannot be used in the same request.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description:
Unique key corresponding to a institution connection's position in the paginated list of results -- used to indicate that only institution connections residing after this key will be retrieved.

Each institution connection carries a unique key. Reference Pagination

The before and after fields cannot be used in the same request.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Limits number of returned institution connections.

Default value: 10

Minimum value: 1

Maximum value: 100

parameters.name: institutionName
parameters.description: Institution name. If present, only institution connections with this institution will be included.If institutionId is present then institutionId will take precedence.

post.description: Update a connection and its products. Adds new products retroactively to an existing connection and triggers a refresh with this updated set of products. The products provided must not already be present on the connection and be supported by the underlying service provider and institution in order to be successful.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: Connection id

202.description: Request for adding products to connection has been accepted

403.description: Unauthenticated or authenticated with insufficient access

500.description: Server Error. This applies to all 5xx errors, including but not limited to 500, 501, 502, 503, and 504 errors. You should treat all of these errors the same.

post.summary: Refresh accounts belonging to a connection
post.description:
Trigger a refresh of all financial accounts for a given institution connection with the latest information from the service provider. This is a premium service for most service providers and should be used sparingly for requesting up-to-date data as of the time of the call to this endpoint. Transaction and balance data is updated daily by service providers, and Meld's webhooks will notify of these updates so that product data does not become stale.

Note: By default, historical transactions are loaded upon initial connection for all providers. This is a premium service for Finicity and MX, so if this is not desired please reach out to Meld support to disable this feature. After doing so, then historical transactions will only be loaded after the first time a refresh is requested for a Finicity/MX connection that has transactions as an available product.

Note: Duplicate provider connections occur when the same customer connects to the same institution using the same login credentials through the same service provider. In such cases, only the most recently refreshed of these duplicates is actively maintained and the rest are considered inactive. Choosing to refresh an inactive duplicate will cause it to become the new active duplicate and it will assume the same connection status as the previous active duplicate. The previous active duplicate will now be considered inactive.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: Connection id

serviceProviderInstitutionId.description: Service provider institution id to search by

icon.description: Url of the icon image for the institution

tile.description: Url of the tile image for the institution

wide.description: Url of the wide image for the institution

aliases.description: List of aliases for the institution

isTest.description: If the institution is a test institution

key.description: The key of the institution in the paginated list of results

rank.description: Institution's popularity score, which is used to rank institutions in the bank linking connect widget

subName.description: Alternate name that may be provided by the service provider

id.description: Service provider's id for the institution

numDeleted.description: Number of institutions deleted. Includes institutions that were previously deleted (because they should still not be included in the published set of institutions).

numPublished.description: Number of institutions updated. Includes new institutions as well as updates to existing institutions.

dayOfMonth.description: The day of the customer's birthday

month.description: The month of the customer's birthday

year.description: The year of the customer's birthday

customerDetails.description: Details about the customer; required for report generation. If a report already exists for the customer, then it is optional to provide for future report generations.

customerId.description: Meld generated unique identifier for your customer. This should be used to track customer activity.

includePending.description: Whether to include pending transactions in the report. Defaults to true

startDate.description: The start date of the report (up to 2 years ago). Defaults to 30 days ago

id.description: The id of the report used for fetching

products.description: List of products to add retroactively to the connection.

List of products to refresh for the connection. Can only select products which were included when the connection was made. Another way to say this is you cannot request a refresh of products the customer has not granted access to. If no products are specified, all products from the connection are refreshed.

serviceProviderDetails.description: Additional Service Provider details, may be null

category.description: The widget type associated with the previously authenticated crypto session

previouslyAuthenticatedId.description:
The Meld id of the crypto session in which the customer has already authenticated with their institution. The auth tied to this session will be used to import the connection if it is still valid. This id may pertain to the initial session in which the customer authenticated, or any subsequent session that used this prior authentication successfully.

For more details, reference Using Multiple Products without Re-authenticating

products.description: Meld products to import from the connection. Optional. If not present, then all products supported by the institution will be used

externalCustomerId.description: Externally assigned id for the customer

products.description: Meld products to import from the connection. Optional. If not present, then all products supported by the institution will be used

importType.description: The type of import being performed.

products.description: Meld products to import from the connection. Optional. If not present, then all products supported by the institution will be used

authenticationBypassDetails.description: Details of the previously authenticated crypto session that will be used to import the connection. Only required if importType is MELD_AUTHENTICATED.

connections.description: Import details for the connections specific to the service provider

serviceProviderDetails.description: Service provider specific details relating to the step. This can include identifying info for the connection so that it can be associated with the Meld connection

accountLogoUrl.description: The path to the account's logo for displaying in the bank picker

borderColor.description: The color of the border for the bank picker

borderTextColor.description: The color of the text on the bank picker border (i.e. 'Integrations powered by Meld.io')

font.description: The desired font to use for the Meld picker

institutionSelectionText.description: The text to display at the top of bank picker

routingRetry.description: Whether to present the option to try to connect to the institution again with a different service provider if the last attempt failed. If a value is not set, it is assumed to be false.

routingRetryBankButtonColor.description: The color of the routing retry button to select a different institution

routingRetryProviderButtonColor.description: The color of the routing retry button to select a different provider

selectionStyle.description: The style in which the bank picker displays the institutions

showBorder.description: Whether to show the border for the bank picker widget. If a value is not set, it is assumed to be false.

accountPreferenceOverride.description: The account preference overrides for this connection

allowRedirect.description:
When searching financial institutions via the Meld bank picker, filters the results based on whether or not the service provider will cause a redirect as part of account linking.

When false, will only include financial institutions which will not cause a redirect. When true, all financial institutions are included regardless of whether or not they may require a redirect. Default is true.

externalCustomerId.description: Your unique identifier for your customer. If maintaining your own customer management system this can also be used for tracking customer activity.

institutionId.description: Institution id to bypass the institution selection pane.

institutionSearchString.description: When searching financial institutions via the Meld bank picker, pre-populate the search string with this value

optionalProducts.description:
Set of products to load from the financial institution via the service provider if supported.

Can be empty as long as there is at least one required product specified. There must be at least one product specified somewhere for the connection to load some data.

Financial institutions do not need to support any of these products in order to be included in the Meld bank picker search results.

products.description:
Set of products to load from the financial institution via the service provider after the connection is completed.

Can be empty as long as there is at least one optional product specified. There must be at least one product specified somewhere for the connection to load some data.

When searching financial institutions via the Meld bank picker, only those which support all of the selected products will be included. When empty, financial institutions will not be filtered by supported products.

Note: Each service provider handles products differently, and vary in which accounts they expose based on the inputted products.

For more details, reference Supported Products and the relevant Provider Details pages, e.g. Plaid

redirectUrl.description: If provided, the url to redirect to after completing the service provider's widget flow

regions.description: When searching financial institutions via the Meld bank picker, onlythose which service any of the regions provided will be included. A service region can be either an ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code or an ISO 3166-2 subdivision code. National financial institutions which service an entire country are included for the corresponding country when only subdivision regions are provided.

connectToken.description: Connect token needed to go through the connect flow

customerId.description: Meld will create a new customer or use the existing customer based on the external customer id provided in the request. Keep track of this customer id for use in other endpoints and webhook tracking.

externalCustomerId.description: External id for the customer as provided in the request

widgetUrl.description: Widget url to go through the connect flow

accountPreferenceOverride.description: The account preference overrides for this connection

allowRedirect.description:
When searching financial institutions via the Meld bank picker, filters the results based on whether or not the service provider will cause a redirect as part of account linking.

When false, will only include financial institutions which will not cause a redirect. When true, all financial institutions are included regardless of whether or not they may require a redirect. Default is true.

externalCustomerId.description: Your unique identifier for your customer. If maintaining your own customer management system this can also be used for tracking customer activity.

institutionSearchString.description: When searching financial institutions via the Meld bank picker, pre-populate the search string with this value

optionalProducts.description:
Set of products to load from the financial institution via the service provider if supported.

Can be empty as long as there is at least one required product specified. There must be at least one product specified somewhere for the connection to load some data.

Financial institutions do not need to support any of these products in order to be included in the Meld bank picker search results.

previousConnectionId.description: Repairs the connection with this id, prompting the user to enter the associated service provider's UI to fix the connection. Must be used in conjunction with the same externalCustomerId, regions, and products that were used in the initial connection.

products.description:
Set of products to load from the financial institution via the service provider after the connection is completed.

Can be empty as long as there is at least one optional product specified. There must be at least one product specified somewhere for the connection to load some data.

When searching financial institutions via the Meld bank picker, only those which support all of the selected products will be included. When empty, financial institutions will not be filtered by supported products.

Note: Each service provider handles products differently, and vary in which accounts they expose based on the inputted products.

For more details, reference Supported Products and the relevant Provider Details pages, e.g. Plaid

redirectUrl.description: If provided, the url to redirect to after completing the service provider's widget flow

regions.description: When searching financial institutions via the Meld bank picker, onlythose which service any of the regions provided will be included. A service region can be either an ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code or an ISO 3166-2 subdivision code. National financial institutions which service an entire country are included for the corresponding country when only subdivision regions are provided.

connectionId.description: The id of the connection in need of repair

connectToken.description: Connect token needed to invoke the repair widget. This will prompt the user to enter the associated service provider's UI to fix the connection.

id.description: Id of the broken connection in need of repair.

institutionId.description: The institution id of the connection to repair. The customer will not need to select an institution again in the picker, but will instead be directed immediately to repair the connection with this institution.

widgetUrl.description: Widget url to go through the repair flow

serviceProvider.description: If provided and supported, return details for this service provider instead of for the best service provider available.

isReconnect.description: Whether this connect session is repairing an existing connection. If true, the FE should not allow the user to connect with another institution/service provider if a failure occurs in the service provider's widget.

otherAvailableServiceProviders.description: Ordered ranking of other viable service providers that can be used to connect to the selected institution. One of these can be passed into the /connect/provider request to override the best service provider available. If empty, there are no remaining service providers capable of establishing a connection to the selected institution with the requested products. If null, this indicates that the account does not have the routingRetry preference enabled and a different institution should be selected upon failure.

redirectUrl.description: If present, the url to redirect to after the connect flow is completed.

redirects.description: Whether the service provider UI will redirect for the institution.

serviceProvider.description: The service provider used for the current connect session.

serviceProviderDetails.description: Service provider specific details needed to start a connection. For Yodlee, this includes the configName of the configuration template used to initialize the connect flow. Note: Not all service providers require additional details to start a connection. This value will be null if additional details are not required.

serviceProviderInitializationErrors.description: Service provider errors that occurred during initialization. If an error occurs, the next service provider in the routing retry ranking will be attempted until there are none remaining. If empty, there were no errors during initialization. If null, then the account doesn't have the routingRetry preference enabled, so any error during initialization would immediately throw an exception instead.

token.description: Token if the service provider needs a token to connect. For Plaid this is a link token. For Yodlee this is an auth token.

url.description: URL if the service provider needs a url to connect. For Finicity this is a connect URL. For Yodlee this is a fast link URL

code.description: The service provider error code thrown

message.description: The service provider error message that occurred during initialization

serviceProvider.description: The service provider that had an error during initialization

serviceProviderDetails.description: The service provider specific details related to the error

accountId.description: The unique identifier of your account with Meld. This id will be the same for all calls to Meld endpoints made from this account.

accountPreference.description: The account preference for this account

algoliaIndexUrl.description: Url to use for querying Algolia for institutions belonging to the index being used

institutionId.description: If present, the institution id to bypass the picker and immediately provide to /connect/provider

institutionSearchString.description: Institution search string to pre-populate the Meld bank picker with

isReconnect.description: Indicates whether this is a reconnect flow. Some providers have different connect complete logic depending on whether this is a reconnect flow or not.

serviceProviderDetails.description: Service provider specific details needed to complete a connection. For Plaid this is a public token. For MX this is a memberGuid that can be captured via postmessages in the widget connect flow. For Yodlee this is a providerAccountId also captured via postmessages in the widget.

processor.description: Processor which will have access to this account

serviceProvider.description: Service provider through which access to the account will be provided. If the account is only connected via one service provider, this field can be omitted and it will use that connected service provider.

token.description: Token for the processor to connect with the service provider for this account

accountId.description: The unique identifier of your account with Meld. This id will be the same for all calls to Meld endpoints made from this account.

connectionIds.description: Ids of connections used to load data for this transaction

customerId.description: Meld generated unique identifier for your customer. This should be used to track customer activity.

externalCustomerId.description: Your unique identifier for your customer. If maintaining your own customer management system this can also be used for tracking customer activity.

id.description: The id of the financial account transaction

key.description: When this financial account transaction is part of a paginated list, this key represents its position in the list

recurringStatus.description: Whether the transaction is a recurring transaction or not. Currently only supported for Plaid or MX connections

serviceProviderDetails.description: Raw service provider details for the service provider transaction(s) associated with this Financial Account Transaction. The raw format varies by service provider, but each result will always contain a serviceProvider field and an updatedAt field. Results are ordered by updatedAt, with the most recent result at the start of the list, and the oldest at the bottom.

remaining.description: Number of remaining institutions before/after this page, depending the direction this page was selected.

status.description: The status of the access profile

count.description: Number of financial account transactions returned.

remaining.description: Number of remaining financial account transactions before/after this page, depending on the direction this page was selected.

createdAt.description: The time the report was initiated with the service provider

customerId.description: Meld generated unique identifier for your customer. This should be used to track customer activity.

serviceProvider.description: The service provider used to generate the report

serviceProviderDetails.description: The raw report details as returned by the service provider

accountId.description: The unique identifier of your account with Meld. This id will be the same for all calls to Meld endpoints made from this account.

connectionIds.description: Ids of connections used to load data for this transaction

customerId.description: Meld generated unique identifier for your customer. This should be used to track customer activity.

externalCustomerId.description: Your unique identifier for your customer. If maintaining your own customer management system this can also be used for tracking customer activity.

id.description: The id of the financial account investment transaction

key.description: When this financial account investment transaction is part of a paginated list, this key represents its position in the list

serviceProviderDetails.description: Raw service provider details for the service provider investment transaction(s) associated with this Financial Account Investment Transaction. The raw format varies by service provider, but each result will always contain a serviceProvider field and an updatedAt field. Results are ordered by updatedAt, with the most recent result at the start of the list, and the oldest at the bottom.

symbol.description: Security identifier. Will be the trading symbol if for publicly traded securities.

count.description: Number of financial account investment transactions returned.

remaining.description: Number of remaining financial account investment transactions before/after this page, depending on the direction this page was selected.

accountId.description: The unique identifier of your account with Meld. This id will be the same for all calls to Meld endpoints made from this account.

closePrice.description: Price of the asset at the close of the previous trading session

currentValue.description: Total value at the close of previous trading session. Null for non-publicly traded securities

customerId.description: Meld generated unique identifier for your customer. This should be used to track customer activity.

externalCustomerId.description: Your unique identifier for your customer. If maintaining your own customer management system this can also be used for tracking customer activity.

id.description: The id of the financial account investment holding

key.description: When this financial account investment holding is part of a paginated list, this key represents its position in the list

quantity.description: Total number of units of the asset held. Depending on the type of the holding, this will be either the number of shares, number of options, or the number of crypto currency tokens held.

serviceProviderDetails.description: Raw service provider details for the service provider investment holdings(s) associated with this Financial Account Investment Holding. The raw format varies by service provider, but each result will always contain a serviceProvider field and an updatedAt field. Results are ordered by updatedAt, with the most recent result at the start of the list, and the oldest at the bottom.

symbol.description: Security identifier. Will be the trading symbol if for publicly traded securities or the crypto currency symbol for crypto holdings.

count.description: Number of financial account investment holdings returned.

remaining.description: Number of remaining financial account investment holdings before/after this page, depending on the direction this page was selected.

accountId.description: The unique identifier of your account with Meld. This id will be the same for all calls to Meld endpoints made from this account.

activeDuplicateConnectionId.description: If duplicate connections exist for this connection, then this specifies which of these duplicates is the actively maintained connection. If the connection has no duplicates, then this will be null. The primary duplicate connection defaults to whichever one was aggregated most recently, but can be manipulated by refreshing another duplicate (in which case that connection assumes the connection status of the active connection, and the previously active connection becomes an INACTIVE_DUPLICATE). Note that the active duplicate will not be guaranteed to have an actual connection status of ACTIVE (it may be RECONNECT_REQUIRED for example), this is just an indicator that it is the main duplicate that is routinely updated.

duplicateConnectionIds.description:
Duplicate connections occur when multiple Meld connections reference the same underlying service provider connection. This occurs when the same customer connects to the same bank account(s) through the same service provider in separate connect sessions using the same login credentials.

Note: Reference Duplicate Connections for more details.

externalCustomerId.description: Your unique identifier for your customer. If maintaining your own customer management system this can also be used for tracking customer activity.

id.description: The id of the institution connection

key.description: When this institution connection is part of a paginated list, this key represents its position in the list

serviceProviderDetails.description: Service provider specific details for the connection

statusReason.description: Reason for the connection status. Applicable primarily for error statuses. Possible values can be found here

successfullyAggregatedAt.description: The last time the connection was successfully aggregated by the underlying service provider. This value will be null until the first aggregation is completed. For service providers that send webhooks only when changes are detected during daily aggregations, this value may become stale if the connection hasn't had new balances or transactions in a while. The service provider may still be successfully aggregating the connection daily, but because no changes are being detected (and thus no webhooks being sent to Meld), then this value may not reflect the true last aggregation time, but rather the last time that changes were detected by the service provider.

remaining.description: Number of remaining connections before/after this page, depending on the direction this page was selected.

accountNumber.description: The ACH account number for the financial account

routingNumber.description: The ACH routing number for the financial account

primary.description: Identifies whether this address is the primary address for the owner

full.description: The full address string. Present for service providers that do not parse the address into parts.

street.description: The street and residence number. Includes the unit number if applicable

accountNumber.description: The BACS account number for the financial account

sortCode.description: The BACS sort code for the financial account

accountNumber.description: The EFT account number for the financial account

branchNumber.description: The EFT branch number for the financial account

institutionNumber.description: The EFT institution number for the financial account

primary.description: Identifies whether this email is the primary email for the owner

accountId.description: The unique identifier of your account with Meld. This id will be the same for all calls to Meld endpoints made from this account.

connectionIds.description: Ids of connections used to load data for this financial account

externalCustomerId.description: Your unique identifier for your customer. If maintaining your own customer management system this can also be used for tracking customer activity.

historicalAggregationSucceeded.description: Whether historical aggregation has succeeded for this financial account.

id.description: The id of the financial account

key.description: When this financial account is part of a paginated list, this key represents its position in the list

serviceProviderDetails.description: Raw service provider details for the service provider account(s) associated with this Financial Account. The raw format varies by service provider, but each result will always contain a serviceProvider field and an updatedAt field. Results are ordered by updatedAt, with the most recent result at the start of the list, and the oldest at the bottom.

subtype.description: Subtype of the financial account. Possible subtypes can be found at Account Types and Subtypes

type.description: Type of the financial account. Possible types can be found at Account Types and Subtypes

availableAmount.description: The amount available to be withdrawn from the financial account as determined by the institution

currentAmount.description: The current amount in the financial account

updatedAt.description: The last time balances were updated for this financial account

ach.description: ACH details for the financial account

bacs.description: BACS details for the financial account

eft.description: EFT details for the financial account

international.description: International details for the financial account

name.description: The name of the financial account as provided by the institution

truncatedAccountNumber.description: The last 4 digits of the account number

addresses.description: The address(es) associated with this owner

emails.description: The email(s) associated with this owner

phoneNumbers.description: The phone number(s) associated with this owner

remaining.description: Number of remaining financial accounts before/after this page, depending on the direction this page was selected.

accountName.description: The Account Name for the financial account

accountNumber.description: The Account Number for the financial account

bban.description: The Basic Bank Account Number (BBAN) for the financial account

bic.description: The Bank Identifier Code (BIC) for the financial account

iban.description: The International Bank Account Number (IBAN) for the financial account

sortCode.description: The Sort Code for the financial account

data.description: The phone number for this owner

primary.description: Identifies whether this phone number is the primary phone number for the owner

accountPreferenceOverride.description: The account preference overrides for this connection

allowRedirect.description:
When searching financial institutions via the Meld bank picker, filters the results based on whether or not the service provider will cause a redirect as part of account linking.

When false, will only include financial institutions which will not cause a redirect. When true, all financial institutions are included regardless of whether or not they may require a redirect. Default is true.

externalCustomerId.description: Your unique identifier for your customer. If maintaining your own customer management system this can also be used for tracking customer activity.

institutionSearchString.description: When searching financial institutions via the Meld bank picker, pre-populate the search string with this value

optionalProducts.description:
Set of products to load from the financial institution via the service provider if supported.

Can be empty as long as there is at least one required product specified. There must be at least one product specified somewhere for the connection to load some data.

Financial institutions do not need to support any of these products in order to be included in the Meld bank picker search results.

products.description:
Set of products to load from the financial institution via the service provider after the connection is completed.

Can be empty as long as there is at least one optional product specified. There must be at least one product specified somewhere for the connection to load some data.

When searching financial institutions via the Meld bank picker, only those which support all of the selected products will be included. When empty, financial institutions will not be filtered by supported products.

Note: Each service provider handles products differently, and vary in which accounts they expose based on the inputted products.

For more details, reference Supported Products and the relevant Provider Details pages, e.g. Plaid

redirectUrl.description: If provided, the url to redirect to after completing the service provider's widget flow

regions.description: When searching financial institutions via the Meld bank picker, onlythose which service any of the regions provided will be included. A service region can be either an ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code or an ISO 3166-2 subdivision code. National financial institutions which service an entire country are included for the corresponding country when only subdivision regions are provided.

eft.description: EFT details for the financial account

international.description: International details for the financial account

subtype.description: Subtype of the financial account. Possible subtypes can be found at Account Types and Subtypes

truncatedAccountNumber.description: The ast 4 digits of the account number

type.description: Type of the financial account. Possible types can be found at Account Types and Subtypes

products.description: List of products to refresh for the connection. Can only select products which were included when the connection was made. Another way to say this is you cannot request a refresh of products the customer has not granted access to. If no products are specified, all products from the connection are refreshed.
